questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: A ball is thrown vertically upward with a speed of 40 m/s. The time taken by the ball to reach the maximum height would be approximately

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following particles in the nucleus of an atom was discovered by J. Chadwick?
questions.solution:
Looking at the history of particle discoveries:

The electron was discovered by J.J. Thomson.

The proton was identified by Ernest Rutherford.

The positron was discovered by Carl Anderson.

The neutron was discovered by James Chadwick.

And among the particles listed, the electron is found outside the nucleus, while protons and neutrons are found inside the nucleus. The positron is an antiparticle and not a standard part of the nucleus.

So, the particle in the nucleus discovered by J. Chadwick is the Neutron. That's option 4!

Chadwick's discovery in 1932 was super important for understanding the atom. He found this neutral particle that had a mass very close to that of a proton, which helped explain the mass of atoms that couldn't just be accounted for by protons alone.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: A glass prism splits white light into different colours. This phenomenon is called dispersion of light by prism. Which one of the following statements is correct?
options: Red light will deviate the most and it is because of the reflection of light
Violet light will deviate the most and it is because of the refraction of light
Red light will deviate the most and it is because of the refraction of light
Violet light will deviate the most and it is because of the reflection of light
questions.solution:
Option B is correct: Violet light will deviate the most and it is because of the refraction of light.

When white light passes through a glass prism, it is dispersed into its constituent colors due to refraction. Each color of light bends at a different angle because each color has a different wavelength and refractive index. Violet light, having the shortest wavelength, is refracted the most, while red light, having the longest wavelength, is refracted the least. This differential bending of light waves causes the splitting of white light into its spectral colors, known as dispersion.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: One parsec in astronomical units (A.U.) is about
questions.solution:
We know:

  • $1 \, \text{parsec} = 3.26 \, \text{light years}$

  • $1 \, \text{light year} \approx 6.3 \times 10^4 \, \text{A.U.}$

So,

$$ 1 \, \text{parsec} = 3.26 \times 6.3 \times 10^4 \, \text{A.U.} $$

$$ \approx 20.5 \times 10^4 \, \text{A.U.} $$

$$ = 2.05 \times 10^5 \, \text{A.U.} $$

So, one parsec is about:

$$ 2 \times 10^5 \, \text{A.U.} $$

Correct option: Option 3

questions.questionText: The commercial unit of electrical energy is kilowatt-hour (kWh), which is equal to

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: At uniform speed the acceleration is
questions.solution:
Option 3: Zero

Since acceleration is the rate of change of velocity, for uniform speed (and straight-line motion) we have

$$a = \frac{dv}{dt} = 0\,. $$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is the usual unit of measurement for Air Pressure used in India?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option B: Millibar. Here's why:

Air pressure is the weight of the atmosphere pressing down on a given area. It's measured as the force per unit area. Here's a breakdown of why the other options are incorrect and why millibars are used:

  • Centimeter (Option A): Centimeters measure length or distance, not pressure.
  • Milligram (Option C): Milligrams measure mass, not pressure.
  • Milliliter (Option D): Milliliters measure volume, not pressure.
  • Millibar (Option B): Millibars are the standard unit of measurement for air pressure in India and many other parts of the world. A millibar is a unit of pressure equal to 1/1000 of a bar.

In India, weather reports and forecasts typically use millibars (mb) to indicate air pressure. Other units of measurement for air pressure exist (like inches of mercury), but millibars are the most common in India.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: <!-- x-tinymce/html -->An astronaut whose weight on the Earth is 600 N experiences weightless- ness on International Space Station orbiting around the Earth. It means that
options: normal reaction of the space- station floor on the astronaut is zero
gravitational pull of earth on the astronaut is zero
space station applies a centrifugal force on the astronaut
questions.solution:
1. On Earth, the weight of the astronaut is

    $$W = mg = 600\text{ N}\quad\bigl(m=\tfrac{W}{g}\bigr).$$

  2. In the International Space Station (ISS) both the station and the astronaut are in continuous free fall around the Earth. They have the same centripetal acceleration towards Earth.

  3. “Weightlessness” means the astronaut feels no contact force from the floor. In free fall:

    – Gravitational force $mg$ acts on the astronaut.

    – There is no normal reaction $N$ from the floor (because both fall together).

  4. Therefore:

    Option 2 is correct – the normal reaction of the space-station floor on the astronaut is zero.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: What is the nature of velocity-time graph for a car moving with uniform acceleration?
questions.solution:
The nature of the velocity-time graph for a car moving with uniform acceleration is a straight line. This is because uniform acceleration means that the velocity of the car changes at a constant rate over time.

Mathematically, if a car is moving with uniform acceleration, the relationship between its velocity $ v $ and time $ t $ can be described by the equation:

$$ v = u + at $$

Where:

  • $ v $ is the final velocity.
  • $ u $ is the initial velocity.
  • $ a $ is the constant acceleration.
  • $ t $ is the time.

This equation is of the form of a linear equation $ y = mx + c $, where $ m $ (the slope) represents the acceleration $ a $, and $ c $ (the y-intercept) represents the initial velocity $ u $. Therefore, the graph of velocity versus time will be a straight line.

Thus, the correct answer is:

Option C: Straight line

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: A stone is thrown horizontally from the top of a 20 m high building with a speed of 12 m/s. It hits the ground at a distance R from the building. Taking g = 10 m/s<sup>2</sup> and neglecting air resistance will give :
questions.solution:
1. Vertical motion

  • Initial vertical velocity $u_y = 0$

  • Height $h = 20\,$m

  • Acceleration $g = 10\,$m/s²

  • Use $$h = \tfrac12\,g\,t^2$$

    so $$20 = \tfrac12\cdot10\cdot t^2 \quad\Longrightarrow\quad t^2 = 4 \quad\Longrightarrow\quad t = 2\ \text{s}$$

  1. Horizontal motion

  • Horizontal speed $v_x = 12\,$m/s (constant)

  • Range $R = v_x \,t = 12 \times 2 = 24\,$m

Answer: R = 24 m (Option C)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText:
Condensation of water vapour into water is influenced by which of the following factor/factors?
1Volume of air
2.Humidity
3.Temperature
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.solution:
Condensation occurs when the partial pressure of water vapour reaches its saturation value. That process can be driven by

changing the air volume (hence its pressure),

the existing humidity (vapour content), and

the temperature (which sets the saturation vapour pressure).

Hence all three factors are involved.

Answer: Option 4 (1, 2 and 3)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: When the pitch of sound increases, which one of the following increases?

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: The AC mains domestic supply current in India changes direction in every
questions.solution:
The domestic AC in India has a frequency of 50 Hz.

One full cycle period:

$$T = \frac{1}{f} = \frac{1}{50}\text{ s} = 0.02\text{ s}$$

It reverses direction every half-cycle:

$$\frac{T}{2} = \frac{1}{2}\times\frac{1}{50}\text{ s} = \frac{1}{100}\text{ s} = 0.01\text{ s}$$

Answer: Option 4, $\tfrac{1}{100}$ s.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: The heating element in an electric iron is usually made of
questions.solution:
The heating element in an electric iron is typically made of a material that has a high resistance to generate sufficient heat when electric current passes through it. Among the options provided, the best choice for this application is Nichrome, which is a nickel-chromium alloy. Nichrome is preferred because it has a high melting point, excellent resistance to oxidation, and stable resistance over a wide range of temperatures.

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option C Nichrome

To elaborate more, when electric current flows through Nichrome, it encounters resistance, converting electrical energy into heat energy. This principle of Joule heating is utilized in various heating applications, such as in electric irons, toasters, and hair dryers. The equation governing this process is given by:

$$Q = I^2 R t$$

where:

  • $$Q$$ is the heat energy produced in joules
  • $$I$$ is the current in amperes
  • $$R$$ is the resistance in ohms
  • $$t$$ is the time in seconds

Using Nichrome allows manufacturers to create reliable and efficient heating elements that can withstand high temperatures and prolonged usage.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText:
Which of the following statements with regard to the phenomenon of the primary rainbow formation by water droplets is/are correct?

1. It involves refraction and one internal reflection of sunlight.

2. It involves refraction of sunlight only.

3. It is formed as the inner bow.

4. It may involve more than one internal reflection as well as refraction of sunlight.

Select the answer using the code given below :
questions.solution:
In order to understand the correct statements regarding the phenomenon of primary rainbow formation by water droplets, we need to delve into the physics of light behavior. A primary rainbow is a result of the interplay between refraction, reflection, and dispersion of sunlight within water droplets. Let's analyze the statements:

1. It involves refraction and one internal reflection of sunlight.

This statement is correct. When sunlight enters a water droplet, it bends (refracts). Inside the droplet, the light reflects off the inner surface and then refracts again as it exits the droplet. This process creates the spectrum of colors seen in a primary rainbow.

2. It involves refraction of sunlight only.

This statement is incorrect. As explained above, the formation of a primary rainbow involves both refraction and internal reflection, not just refraction alone.

3. It is formed as the inner bow.

This statement is correct. The primary rainbow is the inner bow, and its colors are visible in the sequence of red on the outer edge to violet on the inner edge.

4. It may involve more than one internal reflection as well as refraction of sunlight.

This statement is incorrect with regard to the primary rainbow but applies to secondary rainbows. A secondary rainbow is formed by two internal reflections within the water droplet, leading to a relatively fainter bow outside the primary rainbow with reverse color sequence.

Given this analysis, the correct statements are 1 and 3. Therefore, the answer is:

Option B:

1 and 3

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: The magnetic field produced by a current-carrying straight wire at a point outside the wire depends
options: inversely on the distance from it
directly on the distance from it
inversely at short distances and directly at large distances from it
directly on the distance (at short distances) and inversely on the distance (at long distances) from it

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: What happens when the sunlight travels through the Earth’s atmosphere?
options: The blue colour is scattered more compared to the red colour
The red colour is scattered more compared to the blue colour
Both the blue and the red colours are scattered equally
The blue colour is not scattered but the red colour is scattered the most
questions.solution:
Concept:

Scattering of Light: This is a phenomenon where light rays are redirected in various directions when they encounter particles of a size similar to the wavelength of the light.

Rayleigh Scattering: When light scatters off molecules in the air or particles up to about a tenth of the wavelength of the light, it is known as Rayleigh scattering.

According to Rayleigh scattering:

$ \text{Scattering} \propto \frac{1}{{\lambda^4}} $

Where $\lambda$ is the wavelength of light.

For white light, the order of wavelength is:

Violet < Indigo < Blue < Green < Yellow < Orange < Red

Explanation:

Scattering is inversely proportional to the fourth power of the wavelength of light.

Among primary colors (blue, green, yellow, orange, and red), blue light has the shortest wavelength and is therefore scattered the most.

The scattered light in the sky is rich in blue light, which is why the sky appears blue. Hence, the correct conclusion aligns with this explanation.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText:
The figure given below shows the direction of the two forces P and Q acting on a skydiver :

Which one among the following statements is correct about the two forces?
options: Force P is caused by the gravity and force Q is caused by the friction
<!-- x-tinymce/html -->When the force P is bigger than the force Q, the speed of the skydiver remains the same
After the parachute opens, force P remains the same while force Q increases
After the parachute opens, force P decreases while force Q increases
questions.solution:
Forces Acting on a Skydiver

When a skydiver jumps out of an airplane, two primary forces act on them: the force of gravity (P) and the force of air resistance or friction (Q).

The force of gravity (P) pulls the skydiver downward towards the Earth.

The force of air resistance (Q) acts upward in the opposite direction and increases with the skydiver's speed.

Explanation:

When the skydiver's parachute is opened, the following occurs:

Force P (gravity) remains unchanged because it depends on the mass of the skydiver and Earth's gravitational pull, which do not change.

Force Q (air resistance) increases significantly as the parachute expands, increasing the surface area and thereby increasing air resistance.

Thus, after the parachute opens, force P remains the same while force Q increases.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is a non-conventional source of energy?
questions.solution:
Non-conventional (renewable or alternative) sources are things like solar, wind, tidal, geothermal, etc.

• Petroleum, natural gas and coal are all conventional (fossil) fuels.

• Tidal energy harnesses the rise and fall of ocean tides, making it a renewable—hence non-conventional—source.

Answer: Option 3 (Tidal energy).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: A person can see nearby objects clearly but cannot see distant object distinctly, the person is suffering with
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

$$\text{Option 3: Myopia}$$

Step by step:

  • In myopia (short-sightedness), a person can see nearby objects clearly.

  • But distant objects appear blurred.

So the given condition matches myopia.

Final answer:

$$\boxed{\text{Myopia}}$$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText:
Which of the following statements give characteristics of contact forces?

1. It appears between an object when it is in contact with some other object

2. It satisfies the third law of motion

3. It may appear between a pair of solid and fluid

Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.solution:
1. Definition of contact force

    – A force that arises only when two bodies are in physical contact.

  2. Check statement (1): “It appears between an object when it is in contact with some other object”

    – This is exactly the definition of a contact force.

    ⇒ Statement 1 is correct.

  3. Check statement (2): “It satisfies the third law of motion”

    – Newton’s third law, “To every action there is an equal and opposite reaction,” holds for all forces, including contact forces.

    ⇒ Statement 2 is correct.

  4. Check statement (3): “It may appear between a pair of solid and fluid”

    – Examples:

    • Fluid pressure on a submerged plate (normal contact force)

    • Viscous drag between a fluid and a moving solid

    ⇒ Statement 3 is correct.

All three statements are true.

Answer: Option 4 (1, 2 and 3)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: The universal constant of gravitation G has the unit
questions.solution:
The correct choice is Option 4: N·m²/kg².

From Newton’s law of universal gravitation,

$$F = G \frac{m_1\,m_2}{r^2}$$

Solving for G gives

$$G = \frac{F\,r^2}{m_1\,m_2}$$

– Force F has units of newtons (N)

– Distance r has units of meters (m)

– Mass m₁, m₂ have units of kilograms (kg)

So

$$[G] = \frac{\text{N}\times\text{m}^2}{\text{kg}^2}.$$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements about Light year:

1. Light year is a unit for measurement of very large distances.

2. Light year is a unit for measurement of very large time intervals.

3. Light year is a unit for measurement of intensity of light.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.solution:
Only statement 1 is correct.

A light year is a unit of distance, not time or intensity of light. It is defined as the distance that light travels in a vacuum in one year. The speed of light is approximately $299,792,458$ meters per second, so over the course of a year, this distance is about:

$$ 1 \text{ light year} = 9.461 \times 10^{15} \text{ meters} $$

Thus, the correct option is D: 1 only.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText:
Which of the following statements are correct about the electrical resistance and resistivity of a wire?

1. Both quantities depend on the area of cross-section of the wire

2. Both depend on the temperature

3. Resistance of the wire is directly proportional to the resistivity of the wire

4. Resistivity of the wire is directly proportional to the length of the
wire

Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.solution:
To determine the correct statements about the electrical resistance and resistivity of a wire, let's analyze each statement individually.

1. Both quantities depend on the area of cross-section of the wire

This statement is partially correct. The resistance of a wire, given by the formula:

$$ R = \rho \frac{L}{A} $$

depends on the area of cross-section $$A$$, the length $$L$$, and the resistivity $$\rho$$ of the material. However, resistivity $$\rho$$ is an intrinsic property of the material and does not depend on the geometric factors such as the area of cross-section.

2. Both depend on the temperature

This statement is correct. Both resistance $$R$$ and resistivity $$\rho$$ of a wire generally depend on temperature. For most materials, resistivity changes with temperature, and since resistance is proportional to resistivity, resistance also changes with temperature.

3. Resistance of the wire is directly proportional to the resistivity of the wire

This statement is correct. From the equation:

$$ R = \rho \frac{L}{A} $$

it is clear that the resistance $$R$$ is directly proportional to resistivity $$\rho$$ when the length $$L$$ and cross-sectional area $$A$$ are kept constant.

4. Resistivity of the wire is directly proportional to the length of the wire

This statement is incorrect. Resistivity $$\rho$$ is an intrinsic property of the material and does not depend on the length of the wire. The resistivity remains the same regardless of the wire's length.

Based on the analysis, the correct statements are:

2. Both depend on the temperature.

3. Resistance of the wire is directly proportional to the resistivity of the wire.

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option C

2 and 3

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Shown in the figure are two hollow cubes C1 and C2 of negligible mass partially filled (depicted by darkened area) with liquids of densities ρ1 and ρ2, respectively, floating in water (density ρw). The relationship between ρ1, ρ2 and ρw is
options: ρ2 < ρ1 < ρw
ρ1 < ρw < ρ2
questions.solution:
Key Idea:

When something floats, it means it is lighter (has a lower average density) than the liquid it is in.

For floating, the weight of water pushed aside by the object is equal to the weight of the object.

If an object sinks deeper (is more submerged), it means its average density is higher.

For hollow cubes with very small weight, the heaviness (density) of the cube comes only from the liquid that is inside them.

What the Figure Shows:

The figure has two cubes, C1 and C2, each filled with a different liquid. The densities of these liquids are ρ1 and ρ2.

C2 is deeper in the water than C1. This means C2 is heavier (has a greater density) than C1.

Because the cubes themselves do not weigh much, the only thing that affects how deep they float is the density of the liquid inside.

So, the density ρ1 (inside C1) is greater than ρ2 (inside C2).

Both cubes float. This tells us that the density of the liquids inside must be less than the density of water (ρw), or otherwise they would sink.

Putting all this together:

ρ2 < ρ1 < ρw

Answer: ρ2 < ρ1 < ρw

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which among the following are essential constituents of Portland cement?
questions.solution:
Portland cement is a key material in construction and its essential ingredients must contribute to its properties such as strength, setting time, and durability. Let's break down the components of each option to determine the correct answer:

Option A: Sand, lime, clay

While sand, lime, and clay are important materials in construction, not all of these are essential constituents of Portland cement. Sand is an aggregate used in concrete, but it is not a key ingredient in the cement itself.

Option B: Silica, alumina, lime

This option lists silica, alumina, and lime. These are indeed the principal constituents of Portland cement:

  • Silica ($$SiO_2$$): Provides strength to cement.
  • Alumina ($$Al_2O_3$$): Imparts quick setting ability to the cement.
  • Lime ($$CaO$$): Primary ingredient that reacts with water to form calcium silicate hydrates, giving cement its strength.

Option C: Silica, lime, graphite powder

Graphite powder is not a constituent of Portland cement, which makes this option incorrect.

Option D: Sand, graphene, clay

This option includes sand and graphene, neither of which are required in the chemical composition of Portland cement.

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option B: Silica, alumina, lime

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Litmus, a well-known acid-base indicator, is derived from :
questions.solution:
Litmus is a well-known acid-base indicator and is derived from lichens. Lichens are a symbiotic association between a fungus and an alga or cyanobacterium. When lichens are processed, they yield a mixture of dyes which is used to make litmus paper. The primary lichens used for the production of litmus include species from the genera Rocella and Lecanora.

Thus, the correct answer is :

Option B

Lichens

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Vapours of sulphur escaping from a volcano often form a crust on the rocks. The process involved is an example of
questions.solution:
When vapors of sulfur escape from a volcano and form a crust on the rocks, the process involved is known as deposition. Deposition is the phase transition in which a substance goes directly from a gas to a solid without passing through the liquid phase. This is observed when sulfur vapors cool rapidly and solidify on the surfaces of rocks.

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option C: deposition

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which of the hydrocarbons are arranged as per the increasing order of their boiling points?
questions.solution:
To arrange hydrocarbons in order of increasing boiling points, we need to consider their molecular weights and structures. Generally, for alkanes (saturated hydrocarbons), the boiling point increases with the number of carbon atoms due to increased van der Waals forces.

Let's examine each option:

Option A: Methane, Butane, Propane, Heptane

Methane (CH₄) has the lowest boiling point.

Propane (C₃H₈) follows next.

Butane (C₄H₁₀) follows propane.

Heptane (C₇H₁₆) has the highest boiling point.

The order in this option is not correct.

Option B: Propane, Butane, Pentane, Octane

Propane (C₃H₈)

Butane (C₄H₁₀)

Pentane (C₅H₁₂)

Octane (C₈H₁₈)

This order is correct as it lists the hydrocarbons in order of increasing carbon number and boiling point.

Option C: Propane, Butane, Heptane, Methane

Methane should be first, not last, as it has the lowest boiling point.

This order is incorrect.

Option D: Octane, Ethane, Methane, Propane

Octane (C₈H₁₈) should have the highest boiling point, not the lowest.

This order is also incorrect.

Therefore, Option B is the correct choice, arranging the hydrocarbons in order of increasing boiling points: Propane, Butane, Pentane, Octane.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one among the following is present in the nettle leaf hairs that causes burning pain?
questions.solution:
The substance present in nettle leaf hairs that causes a burning pain is Methanoic acid. Also known as formic acid, methanoic acid is responsible for the stinging sensation experienced when one comes into contact with nettle leaves.

So, the correct answer is:

Option A

Methanoic acid

Methanoic acid, or formic acid, has the molecular formula $$HCOOH$$. This compound is known for its ability to irritate and cause pain upon skin contact.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one among the following compounds has a sweet and fruity smell?
questions.solution:
Among the given compounds, the one that has a sweet and fruity smell is Ethyl acetate.

Option A: Ethyl alcohol (ethanol) has a characteristic smell often associated with alcoholic beverages and is not typically described as fruity.

Option B: Acetic acid has a strong, pungent vinegar-like smell.

Option C: Ethyl acetate is known for its sweet, fruity aroma and is commonly used in perfumes, as well as in flavorings and as a solvent. Its molecular formula is $$\text{CH}_3\text{COOCH}_2\text{CH}_3$$ and it is an ester, which are often known for their pleasant, fruity smells.

Option D: Acetophenone has a sweet but more intense smell, often described as reminiscent of almonds or cherries, but not typically purely fruity.

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option C: Ethyl acetate

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is NOT a heterogeneous mixture ?
options: Mixture of sugar 8 and salt crystals
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

$$\boxed{\text{Option 1: Sulphur in carbon disulphide}}$$

Step-by-step:

A heterogeneous mixture has non-uniform composition, and its different parts can be seen separately.

Now check each option:

  1. Sulphur in carbon disulphide

    Sulphur dissolves completely in carbon disulphide, so it forms a homogeneous mixture, not a heterogeneous mixture.

  2. Mixture of sugar and salt crystals

    Sugar crystals and salt crystals remain separate, so it is a heterogeneous mixture.

  3. Sandy water

    Sand does not dissolve in water, so it is a heterogeneous mixture.

  4. Sulphur in water

    Sulphur does not dissolve in water, so it is a heterogeneous mixture.

Therefore, the one which is not a heterogeneous mixture is:

$$\boxed{\text{Sulphur in carbon disulphide}}$$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: The number of structural isomers of pentane is
questions.solution:
The correct answer is 3.

Isomers:

  • Chemical compounds that have identical chemical formulae but differ in properties and the arrangement of atoms in the molecule are called isomers.
    • Therefore, the compounds that exhibit isomerism are known as isomers.
  • Such structural isomers, which differ in the chain of carbon atoms, are known as chain isomers.

Pentane:

  • Pentane is an organic compound that is a clear, colourless liquid.
  • The odour of pentane is similar to petroleum.
  • It is less dense than water and is insoluble in water. It is also known as quintane and n-pentane.
  • The rotatable bond count of pentane is 2.
  • Molecular Formula: C5H12.

Isomers of Pentane: There are three structural isomers of pentane:

  • n-pentane is the first isomer, which is a straight chain with a normal structure of pentane.

  • 2-methylbutane is the second isomer, which is a branched-chain structure in which one carbon atom is connected to three other carbon atoms.

  • 2,2-dimethylpropane is the third isomer, which is a branched-chain structure with the central carbon atom connected to four other carbon atoms.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which of the following anions has a valency of -3 ?
questions.solution:
1. Valency of an anion = magnitude of its charge.

  2. Write the formula and charge for each:

    a. Nitride: $N^{3-}$ ⇒ valency = 3 (charge = –3)

    b. Nitrate: $NO_{3}^{-}$ ⇒ valency = 1 (charge = –1)

    c. Sulphide: $S^{2-}$ ⇒ valency = 2 (charge = –2)

    d. Sulphate: $SO_{4}^{2-}$ ⇒ valency = 2 (charge = –2)

  3. The only anion with valency 3 (charge –3) is nitride.

Answer: Option 1 (Nitride).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is correct?
options: Pyroxene is commonly found in meteorites
Amphiboles form about 20% of the Earth's crust
The word 'sedimentary' is derived from the Latin word kelimentum
questions.solution:
The correct statement is Option 1: Pyroxene is commonly found in meteorites.

• Many stony (chondritic) meteorites are dominated by silicate minerals—primarily olivine and pyroxene.

• Amphiboles make up only a few percent (not ~20%) of the Earth’s crust minerals.

• Quartz is essentially insoluble in pure water.

• “Sedimentary” comes from the Latin sedimentum (“a settling”), not “kelimentum.”

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following metals can be extracted using carbon as reducing agent?
questions.solution:
To determine which metal can be extracted using carbon as a reducing agent, we need to consider the reactivity of the metals. Carbon is typically used to reduce the oxides of metals that are less reactive than carbon itself in the reactivity series. Here's a brief explanation for each metal mentioned in the options:

Option A: Zinc

Zinc is generally extracted from its ore using carbon. The chemical equation for the reduction of zinc oxide by carbon can be written as:

$$ \text{ZnO} + \text{C} \rightarrow \text{Zn} + \text{CO} $$

Here, carbon acts as a reducing agent, converting zinc oxide (ZnO) to zinc (Zn).

Option B: Silver

Silver is usually extracted by other methods such as the amalgamation process or cyanide process rather than using carbon. This is because silver is already quite unreactive and exists in nature mostly in its elemental form or in ores that do not require reduction by carbon.

Option C: Gold

Gold is even more unreactive than silver and is usually found in its native state. Therefore, it is not extracted using carbon. Instead, processes like cyanidation are employed to extract gold from its ores.

Option D: Aluminum

Aluminum is a highly reactive metal and cannot be extracted by reduction with carbon. Instead, it is extracted using electrolysis of aluminum oxide in the Hall-Héroult process.

Based on this analysis, the metal that can be extracted using carbon as a reducing agent is:

Option A: Zinc

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following reactions is an example of decomposition reaction?
options: CH4 (g) + 2O2 (g) → CO2(g) + 2H2O(g)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements about the cleansing action of soap is not true?
options: The oil and dirt gets collected in the centre of the micelle
Soaps are ammonium salts of long chain carboxylic acids
Soap forms insoluble precipitates with the calcium and magnesium ions in hard water
questions.solution:
Option C is not true. Soaps are actually sodium or potassium salts of long chain carboxylic acids, not ammonium salts.

Here's an elaboration on the cleansing action of soap:

Soaps are made from fats and oils, which are turned into soap molecules through a process called saponification. These molecules contain a hydrophobic (water-repelling) tail and a hydrophilic (water-attracting) head.

When soap is added to water, the hydrophobic tails embed themselves in oils and greases, while the hydrophilic heads remain in the water. This forms a structure known as a micelle.

The dirt and oil are trapped in the center of these micelles, allowing them to be washed away with water, effectively cleansing the skin or clothes.

Option B: Soap micelles can scatter light because they are sufficiently large structures in a solution.

Option D: Soaps react with calcium and magnesium ions in hard water to form an insoluble precipitate, commonly known as "soap scum," which can hinder the effective cleaning action of the soap.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following reactions does not result in the evolution of hydrogen gas?
options: Reaction of zinc metal with dilute sulphuric acid solution
Mixing water to Plaster of Paris
Heating zinc metal with sodium hydroxide solution
Reaction of potassium metal with water
questions.solution:
Mixing water with Plaster of Paris does not result in the evolution of hydrogen gas. Plaster of Paris, chemically known as calcium sulfate hemihydrate ($\text{CaSO}_4 \cdot \frac{1}{2}\text{H}_2\text{O}$), when mixed with water undergoes a hydration reaction to form gypsum ($\text{CaSO}_4 \cdot 2\text{H}_2\text{O}$). This process is an exothermic reaction, but it does not produce hydrogen gas.

The chemical equation for this reaction is:

$$ \text{CaSO}_4 \cdot \frac{1}{2}\text{H}_2\text{O} + \frac{3}{2}\text{H}_2\text{O} \rightarrow \text{CaSO}_4 \cdot 2\text{H}_2\text{O} $$

Thus, option B is the correct choice.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements about X-rays is not true?
options: They have wavelengths of about 1 Å.
These can be generated by bombarding a metal target by high energy electrons.
Due to their wavelengths being shorter, these can be used for radar systems.
These are also used for the treatment of certain forms of cancer.
questions.solution:
The statement that is not true about X-rays is:

Option C: Due to their wavelengths being shorter, these can be used for radar systems.

X-rays, due to their very small wavelengths (on the order of 1 Å), are not suitable for radar systems. Radar systems typically use radio waves or microwaves, which have much longer wavelengths. The shorter wavelengths of X-rays make them suitable for imaging and medical applications, but not for radar.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: The intermixing of particles of two different types of matter on their own is called
questions.solution:
Option 3: Diffusion

  1. Definition

    • Diffusion is the spontaneous movement and intermixing of particles of two different substances from a region of higher concentration to a region of lower concentration, without any external force.

  2. Why not the others

    • Osmosis – diffusion of solvent (usually water) through a semipermeable membrane, not general intermixing.

    • Brownian motion – random zig-zag movement of colloidal particles in a fluid, caused by collisions with molecules; does not itself describe mixing of two substances.

    • Conductance – ability of a material to conduct electricity, unrelated to particle mixing.

  3. Key point

    In diffusion, particles move down their concentration gradient until uniform distribution is reached.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one among the following is known as Milk of Magnesia?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option D: Magnesium hydroxide.

Magnesium hydroxide is commonly known as Milk of Magnesia. It has the chemical formula $$\text{Mg(OH)}_2$$. This compound forms a suspension in water that appears milky, which is why it gets the name "Milk of Magnesia." It is often used as an antacid to neutralize stomach acid and as a laxative to relieve constipation.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is the chemical formula of Hypobromous acid?
questions.solution:
The chemical formula for Hypobromous acid is:

Option B: $$ \text{HOBr} $$

Hypobromous acid is a weak, unstable acid and is a bromine analog of hypochlorous acid. It can be represented as $$ \text{HOBr} $$, where the hydrogen atom is covalently bonded to an oxygen atom, which is in turn bonded to a bromine atom. This compound is typically formed by the reaction of bromine with water.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one among the following elements is known to be discovered the earliest?
questions.solution: Copper. Archaeological evidence shows native copper was in use by around 9000 BCE, well before gold (≈6000 BCE), oxygen (isolated in 1774), or uranium (identified in 1789).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText:
In the following redox reaction, which compound is acting as a reducing agent ?

N2H4 + 2H2O2 → N2 + 4H2O
questions.solution:
To find the reducing agent, we check which substance gets oxidised.

Given reaction:

$$ N_2H_4 + 2H_2O_2 \rightarrow N_2 + 4H_2O $$

Step 1: Find oxidation state of nitrogen in $N_2H_4$

Let oxidation state of nitrogen be $x$.

In $N_2H_4$:

  • Hydrogen has oxidation state $+1$

So,

$$ 2x + 4(+1) = 0 $$

$$ 2x + 4 = 0 $$

$$ 2x = -4 $$

$$ x = -2 $$

So, oxidation state of nitrogen in $N_2H_4$ is $-2$.

Step 2: Find oxidation state of nitrogen in $N_2$

In elemental form,

$$ \text{Oxidation state of } N \text{ in } N_2 = 0 $$

So nitrogen changes from:

$$ -2 \rightarrow 0 $$

This is oxidation.

Step 3: Identify reducing agent

The substance which gets oxidised acts as the reducing agent.

Here, $N_2H_4$ is oxidised to $N_2$.

Therefore, the reducing agent is:

$$ \boxed{N_2H_4} $$

Correct option: 2. $N_2H_4$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Toothpaste prevents tooth decay by:
options: making a coat of calcium over the teeth surface.
questions.solution:
Toothpaste helps prevent tooth decay primarily through:

Option C: the action of fluoride.

Fluoride in toothpaste helps to strengthen tooth enamel and make it more resistant to the acids produced by bacteria in the mouth, which can cause decay. Additionally, some toothpastes may help to neutralize acids (Option A), but the primary mechanism is through the remineralization effect of fluoride.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: What is the maximum number of electrons that can occupy the first shell of an atom?
questions.solution:
The maximum number of electrons that can occupy the first shell of an atom is 2.

This is determined by a general rule in atomic structure: the maximum number of electrons a principal energy shell can hold is given by the formula $$2n^2$$ where 'n' represents the principal quantum number (or the shell number).

For the first shell:

$n = 1$

Plugging this into the formula, we get: $$2 \times (1)^2 = 2 \times 1 = 2$$

So, the first shell can hold a maximum of 2 electrons!
